what_it_does: Defines the Operations workspace UI for a live project, including dashboard KPIs, event filtering, timeline milestones, insights, scheduled reports, and live publication management views. It uses global data from window.MS_DATA.opsData, shared UI components, local SVG/CSS chart primitives, and ViContext updates for workspace state.
observations: Exports workspace screens by assigning React components to window.OpsDashboard, window.OpsEvents, window.OpsTimeline, window.OpsInsights, window.OpsReports, and window.OpsManage.
Uses local Spark and Bars primitives instead of an external charting library.
Aliases React hooks as useStateOps and useEffectOps at file scope.
The shared liveHead helper builds consistent Operations headers and reads the live URL from window.MS_DATA.opsData.url.
OpsEvents provides local kind filtering across live event rows.
OpsManage presents publication, access, audience, update, and notice controls, with several switches and buttons rendered as UI controls.
standouts: The file covers the full post-launch Operations surface in one local module.
The visuals are data-driven but intentionally lightweight, using inline styles, CSS variables, and simple SVG/flex layouts.
The workspace includes interpretive Vi notes that connect operational metrics to project workflow decisions.
conventions: Uses global window namespaces for workspace component registration and data access.
Uses shared component-library primitives such as Panel, Chip, Btn, ViNote, Icon, and shared row/card classes.
Keeps many layout decisions inline with CSS variables for spacing, color, typography, and radius.
Uses useEffect calls to set ViContext when each workspace view mounts.
Uses small local presentational helpers for repeated chart, row, card, toggle, and header patterns.
concerns: Spark assumes a non-empty data array and divides by data.length - 1, which can produce invalid coordinates for empty or single-point data.
liveHead accepts a project argument but does not use it.
Several controls render without action handlers or persistence, including refresh/export/report/edit/publish/toggle flows.
OpsToggle manages only local visual state and does not communicate changes outside the component.
The file depends on multiple globals without local guards, including window.MS_DATA, window.ViContext, and shared UI symbols.
notes: This file is the live-operations surface for monitoring, interpreting, reporting on, and lightly managing a shipped project.
questions: Should the live management controls persist changes anywhere, or are they only prototype interactions in this file?
Should liveHead use the project argument or is the live URL intentionally sourced only from global ops data?
What data shape guarantees exist for ops.kpis, ops.events, ops.funnel, ops.milestones, and ops.reports?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file builds a live Operations zone with Dashboard, Events, Timeline, Insights, Reports, and Manage views using shared components plus local chart primitives.
apparent_themes: workflow continuity from Studio to Build to Operations
intention_signals: It appears intended to make the shipped project's state visible and actionable through compact dashboards, filtered activity, trend views, reports, publication controls, and contextual Vi guidance.
novelty: The file blends operational analytics with project-workflow guidance, using very small local chart components and narrative Vi notes rather than a heavier analytics framework.
contribution: This file contributes the live Operations workspace: a set of post-launch views, lightweight metric visualizations, event and report presentation patterns, publication-management controls, and contextual interpretation prompts tied to live project data.
summary_for_embedding: React operations.jsx defines global Operations workspace screens for live project dashboarding, events, timeline, insights, reports, and management using window.MS_DATA.opsData, shared UI components, inline styles, and local chart primitives.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting and managing the state of a shipped project after launch, combining operational metrics, live activity, report workflows, publication controls, and Vi interpretation notes.

symbols.does: Renders a small SVG sparkline for numeric trend data with tone-based stroke color.
symbols.description: Spark computes min, max, normalized SVG polyline points, and a final-point marker from the provided data array. It supports gold, warning, and down/error tones through CSS variables and is used for compact KPI trend display.

symbols.does: Renders a simple vertical bar chart from numeric data and optional labels.
symbols.description: Bars uses a flex row aligned to the bottom, scales each bar against the maximum value, and styles bars with a gold gradient and glow. Labels are displayed beneath bars when provided.

symbols.does: Renders a local-state switch button initialized from an on prop.
symbols.description: OpsToggle presents an accessible role=switch control with aria-checked and a sliding thumb. Its state is contained inside the component, so toggling changes the visual state without calling an external handler.

symbols.does: Builds the shared Operations page header with eyebrow text, title, live chip, URL, and actions.
symbols.description: liveHead returns a ws-head header used across Operations views. It displays the passed subtitle and title, renders supplied action buttons, and reads the live URL directly from window.MS_DATA.opsData.url.

symbols.does: Renders one live event row with an icon, actor, description, and relative time.
symbols.description: OpsEventRow maps event kind values to icon names and event tone values to row icon styling. It displays who performed the event, what happened, and when it happened in the compact qa-row pattern.

symbols.does: Renders a compact insight card with tone chip, title, and body text.
symbols.description: InsightCard chooses a Chip kind and icon from the tone prop, then presents the insight title and explanatory body. It is used by OpsInsights to show operational interpretations such as wins, watch items, and notes.
